req.title: Approve leave request — variant 2
req.statement: A manager shall approve or reject leave requests assigned to them within 5 business days; unactioned requests auto-escalate to hr-admin. Additionally, the action must be idempotent under retry.

doc.reasoning: The requirement covers: (1) a manager approving a valid assigned leave request — positive/EP; (2) a non-assignee manager attempting approval — negative/NEG returning 403; (3) idempotent retry on an already-approved request — boundary/STC returning 409 on second call; (4) decision-table coverage of approve vs. reject + audit entry; (5) BVA on the 5-business-day auto-escalation boundary. Each test uses fixtures for auth tokens and request setup, keeping executables under 40 lines.

tests.name: test_manager_approves_assigned_request_positive
preconditions: Authenticated manager session token available via <manager_auth_token>
A leave request in state=pending exists assigned to this manager, ID via <create_pending_request>
expected: audit log contains entry with action='approved'
tests.executable:
import pytest
from <helper_fn> import api_client, create_pending_request, get_audit_log, get_notifications

def test_manager_approves_assigned_request_positive(<manager_auth_token>, <employee_id>):
    request_id = create_pending_request(assignee_token=<manager_auth_token>, employee_id=<employee_id>)
    client = api_client(token=<manager_auth_token>)

    resp = client.post(f"/leave-requests/{request_id}/approve")
    assert resp.status_code == 200, f"Expected 200, got {resp.status_code}"

    state_resp = client.get(f"/leave-requests/{request_id}")
    assert state_resp.json()["state"] == "approved"

    notifications = get_notifications(employee_id=<employee_id>, token=<manager_auth_token>)
    assert any(n["resource_id"] == request_id for n in notifications)

    audit = get_audit_log(resource_id=request_id, token=<manager_auth_token>)
    assert any(e["action"] == "approved" for e in audit)

tests.name: test_non_assignee_manager_approve_returns_403
preconditions: Authenticated manager session token available via <manager_auth_token> (manager A)
A second manager token available via <other_manager_auth_token> (manager B)
A leave request in state=pending assigned to manager A, ID via <create_pending_request>
actions: POST /leave-requests/{id}/approve with manager B auth token (non-assignee)
expected: response body contains error code indicating forbidden/not-assignee
trace: R-WEB-063: error pathway — 403 if manager not the assignee
tests.executable:
import pytest
from <helper_fn> import api_client, create_pending_request

def test_non_assignee_manager_approve_returns_403(<manager_auth_token>, <other_manager_auth_token>, <employee_id>):
    request_id = create_pending_request(assignee_token=<manager_auth_token>, employee_id=<employee_id>)
    non_assignee_client = api_client(token=<other_manager_auth_token>)

    resp = non_assignee_client.post(f"/leave-requests/{request_id}/approve")
    assert resp.status_code == 403, f"Expected 403, got {resp.status_code}"

    body = resp.json()
    assert "error" in body or "message" in body

    assignee_client = api_client(token=<manager_auth_token>)
    state_resp = assignee_client.get(f"/leave-requests/{request_id}")
    assert state_resp.json()["state"] == "pending"

tests.name: test_duplicate_approve_is_idempotent_returns_409
preconditions: Authenticated manager session token available via <manager_auth_token>
A leave request in state=pending assigned to this manager, ID via <create_pending_request>
expected: Second POST returns HTTP 409 with already-actioned error
request state remains approved (not corrupted by retry)
audit log contains exactly one approved entry
trace: R-WEB-063: action must be idempotent under retry
R-WEB-063: error pathway — 409 if already actioned
R-WEB-063: state transition pending -> approved only once
tests.executable:
import pytest
from <helper_fn> import api_client, create_pending_request, get_audit_log

def test_duplicate_approve_is_idempotent_returns_409(<manager_auth_token>, <employee_id>):
    request_id = create_pending_request(assignee_token=<manager_auth_token>, employee_id=<employee_id>)
    client = api_client(token=<manager_auth_token>)

    first = client.post(f"/leave-requests/{request_id}/approve")
    assert first.status_code == 200, f"First call expected 200, got {first.status_code}"

    second = client.post(f"/leave-requests/{request_id}/approve")
    assert second.status_code == 409, f"Second call expected 409, got {second.status_code}"

    state_resp = client.get(f"/leave-requests/{request_id}")
    assert state_resp.json()["state"] == "approved"

    audit = get_audit_log(resource_id=request_id, token=<manager_auth_token>)
    approved_entries = [e for e in audit if e["action"] == "approved"]
    assert len(approved_entries) == 1, f"Expected 1 audit entry, found {len(approved_entries)}"

tests.name: test_approve_vs_reject_decision_table
preconditions: Authenticated manager session token available via <manager_auth_token>
Two leave requests in state=pending assigned to this manager, IDs via <create_pending_request>
actions: GET both requests to verify final states
GET audit log for both requests
expected: Approve call returns HTTP 200; request id1 state == 'approved'
Reject call returns HTTP 200; request id2 state == 'rejected'
Audit log for id1 has action='approved'
Audit log for id2 has action='rejected'
trace: R-WEB-063: manager shall approve OR reject leave requests
R-WEB-063: audit entry created for each action
R-WEB-063: both terminal states produce correct transitions
tests.executable:
import pytest
from <helper_fn> import api_client, create_pending_request, get_audit_log

def test_approve_vs_reject_decision_table(<manager_auth_token>, <employee_id>):
    id1 = create_pending_request(assignee_token=<manager_auth_token>, employee_id=<employee_id>)
    id2 = create_pending_request(assignee_token=<manager_auth_token>, employee_id=<employee_id>)
    client = api_client(token=<manager_auth_token>)

    r_approve = client.post(f"/leave-requests/{id1}/approve")
    assert r_approve.status_code == 200
    assert client.get(f"/leave-requests/{id1}").json()["state"] == "approved"

    r_reject = client.post(f"/leave-requests/{id2}/reject")
    assert r_reject.status_code == 200
    assert client.get(f"/leave-requests/{id2}").json()["state"] == "rejected"

    audit1 = get_audit_log(resource_id=id1, token=<manager_auth_token>)
    assert any(e["action"] == "approved" for e in audit1)

    audit2 = get_audit_log(resource_id=id2, token=<manager_auth_token>)
    assert any(e["action"] == "rejected" for e in audit2)

tests.name: test_unactioned_request_auto_escalates_at_5_business_days
preconditions: Authenticated manager session token available via <manager_auth_token>
A leave request created exactly 5 business days ago in state=pending assigned to manager, ID via <create_aged_request(days=5)>
actions: Trigger escalation check (via <trigger_escalation_job> or advance system clock)
GET /leave-requests/{id} as hr-admin to verify escalation
expected: Request assignee changes to hr-admin role or escalation flag is set
Request state is 'escalated' or assignee == hr-admin
Audit log contains action='escalated' at the 5-business-day mark
trace: R-WEB-063: unactioned requests auto-escalate to hr-admin within 5 business days
BVA: boundary at exactly 5 business days (inclusive upper bound)
tests.executable:
import pytest
from <helper_fn> import api_client, create_aged_request, trigger_escalation_job, get_audit_log

def test_unactioned_request_auto_escalates_at_5_business_days(<hr_admin_auth_token>, <manager_auth_token>, <employee_id>):
    request_id = create_aged_request(assignee_token=<manager_auth_token>, employee_id=<employee_id>, business_days=5)

    trigger_escalation_job()

    admin_client = api_client(token=<hr_admin_auth_token>)
    state_resp = admin_client.get(f"/leave-requests/{request_id}")
    data = state_resp.json()
    assert state_resp.status_code == 200
    assert data.get("state") == "escalated" or data.get("assignee_role") == "hr-admin"

    audit = get_audit_log(resource_id=request_id, token=<hr_admin_auth_token>)
    assert any(e["action"] == "escalated" for e in audit)
